task_completion.description: Verify the dashboard named 'Web_Error_Investigator' (or 'web_error_investigator') has been created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Splunk Web UI should be visible with the dashboard title 'Web_Error_Investigator' displayed at the top.

task_completion.description: Verify a Text Input exists with the token 'ip_filter' and a default value of '*'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the dashboard edit mode, input configuration modal, or source XML view, look for a text input element assigned the token 'ip_filter' and a default value set to '*'.

task_completion.description: Verify a Dropdown Input exists with the token 'level_filter', a default value of '*', and choices including '*' and 'error'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the dashboard edit mode, input configuration modal, or source XML view, look for a dropdown element assigned the token 'level_filter', with default '*' and options that include '*' and 'error'.

task_completion.description: Verify a Time Picker input is included on the dashboard.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Time Picker control (e.g., showing 'Last 24 hours', 'All time', etc.) should be visible alongside the other inputs at the top of the dashboard.

task_completion.description: Verify the dashboard contains a panel with a search query targeting 'index=web_logs'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the panel's search configuration, edit mode, or source XML view, the search string must explicitly contain 'index=web_logs'.

task_completion.description: Verify the panel's search query dynamically incorporates the '$ip_filter$' and '$level_filter$' tokens.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the panel's search configuration, edit mode, or source XML view, the search string must contain both '$ip_filter$' and '$level_filter$' to filter the results dynamically.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the Splunk Web UI to create and configure the dashboard.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Splunk web interface (http://localhost:8000) in a browser, rather than just terminal commands or API scripts.

integrity.description: Verify the dashboard is genuinely rendered within Splunk.
integrity.visual_evidence: The inputs and panels should be rendered as interactive Splunk UI components, not just text in a file or a mock-up.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not simply type the expected XML into a standalone text editor without applying it to Splunk.
integrity.visual_evidence: If XML is shown, it must be within the Splunk 'Source' editor view for the dashboard, not in an external text editor like nano, vim, or gedit.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The dashboard ID must be 'web_error_investigator'. The text input must use the token 'ip_filter'. The dropdown input must use the token 'level_filter'. The search query in the panel must query 'index=web_logs' and include both tokens.
